entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere, SAP BW bridge: Demystifying the Remote Conversion
entry.content:
Hi Felipe,

Thanks for your response. Apologies for the lengthy comments section :), captured a consolidated list of questions that were not clear to me and was looking for clarifications from experts.

1) Scenario #1- Current BW4H environment uses Hybrid modelling(ADSO + Calc Views) at the Composite provider? If we go with Remote Migration using SAP BW Bridge. Since the calc views cannot be migrated? if i try to select Composite provider and its underlying objects in the transfer cockpit, Will this migration fail as the calc views cannot be generated? Is my understanding accurate? What is SAP's recommended approach in this scenario?

Scenario #2- An object that is commonly used across multiple Composite providers. Ihave an ADSO that is used across Multiple Composite providers? Started capturing the scope for the First Composite provider, the underlying ADSO showed up as dependent obj

When starting with a second Composite provider, do i again need to capture the common ADSO or should i remove it manually? or wIll the system take care of it to avoid object duplicity? How does this work?

Scenario#3 - Identify the Start Object(ADSO/Composite provider) and the tool identifies its dependent objects in the scope identification. We capture it in a package. Moving to the next object and its dependenies, should this also be captured in the same package. if so, how do we identify any errors that need to addressed if all the objects go under 1 packaged. Lets say i have 50 Composite providers, do i capture all them in one package, this package would have all the project scope? If so how do we look into any errors in case of any failure or the next steps?

Any mechanism or process in place to ensure these objects are segregated for further analysis in case of failure? What is SAP's recommendation?

Scenario #4 - What if the underlying objects cannot be migrated, will the start object picked fail? BW4H dataflow with Hybrid modelling

Datasource-->ADSO & Calc View ---> Composite Provider-->BW Query.

What should my start object be in this case to capture the dependent objects As i understand the Calc views cannot be migrated, if calc views are missing then the Composite provider will fail as Calc views references are missing. Also  since  CP fails then the query will fail as it does not have CP reference

Scenario #5- Currently have BO reports on BW4H? Can we even migrate  existing  BO reports to SAC? What is SAP's recommendation for exisiting customer with BO reports on BW4H to be migrated to SAP Datasphere. Any options to migrate BO reports to SAC? As the underlying BW artifacts will be migrated to Datasphere

Scenario#6- Post confirmation of the migration scope. Any tool/estimator that we can leverage to come up with an estimated effort. Any recommended approach on how we can drive this or other insights.

Greatly appreciate your expert insights to clarify my doubts.

entry.title: Batch Job sporadically cancels with error "Object requested is currently locked by user CONTROLM"
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a custom program which updates a field in Table VEKP. The program is run in background mode in Production every 5 mins with user CONTROLM. It usually completes within 2 mins so there is never an overlap of 2 batch jobs of the same program. But, sporadically (5 times out of 120) the batch job fails because of lock issue. 

I tried to check for other batch jobs by CONTROLM which updates VEKP in the same period but didn't find any.

I ran an Enqueue trace in ST12. The trace only shows the program name as "SAPLSENA"  against the Object "EVVEKPE" without fully displaying the ABAP Call Heirarchy.

I do not have access to SM12 in Production yet. Is there a better solution than checking the lock entries while waiting for the batch job to cancel? How do I catch which program run by CONTROLM is causing this issue? 

Best Regards,

Laxman

entry.title: Re: Import on-premise S/4HANA transports into S/4HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Currently we don't have a feature that allows transports between S/4HANA on-premise and cloud. However, you can use abapGit.

AbapGit is an open source developed Git client for the ABAP server to import and export ABAP objects between ABAP systems. With abapGit, you can export your ABAP objects from any system to another, typically from on-premise to cloud or from one cloud system to another.

Kindly check the following documentations:

  • SAP Help Portal | Working with abapGit
  • SAP Help Portal | Key User Extensibility and abapGit
  • SAP Help Portal | Import Content from abapGit Repository into the ABAP Environment 

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

3. What is the role of LLM in this context? SAP partners with many providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Gemini, etc. Are these LLMs powering Joule in any specific way or just to make it conversable (as in chat model) because users need response in natural language.?

Look forward to seeing your feedback..

Thanks, Ram

entry.title: Is it possible to add SAP systems with the same SID to a SAP Solution Manager system?
entry.content:
How to add multiple SAP systems with the same SID to a single SAP Solution Manager system during a migration from on premise system into the Hyperscaler like Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add SAP systems with the same SID t...
entry.content:
Dear,

Assuming a system XXX is installed on one server, then after a "copy" of the source system to an Azure infrastructure, over a different DB Hostname server, correct? So, this will generate a different system number for this second XXX system.

Following that, by checking the resolution section provided in KBA 3417361:

  • “As SID, Installation Number, and DB Hostname are a system's key entries, please ensure that the key entries (Installation Number & DB Hostname) are not the same. SAP Solution Manager can manage the systems with the same SID in this scenario. An extended SID is automatically generated for the second or the third system to distinguish the difference.”

Here are the steps you need to take:

  1. Ensure that the key entries (Installation Number & DB Hostname) are not the same for the two systems. This is because SID, Installation Number, and DB Hostname are a system's key entries.
  2. SAP Solution Manager can manage the systems with the same SID in this scenario. An extended SID is automatically generated for the second or the third system to distinguish the difference.
  3. At LMDB level, there will be two different technical systems XXX and XXX00001.
  4. ChaRM is using the configuration item of the production system in the change documents. Therefore, only when the production system gets replaced, you will need to take care of changing the configuration item of the production system.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: ABAP Program to Upload PDF Form to OneDrive
entry.content:
I suppose you are trying to implement it in S4 on premise .
To upload a PDF file to a OneDrive folder using a shared link in ABAP, you can leverage the Microsoft Graph API for OneDrive and the Harmonized Document Management (HDM) concept in S/4HANA. Below is a sample ABAP code snippet to help you get started:

  1. Set up the Microsoft Graph API: You need to register your application in the Azure portal to get the necessary credentials (client ID, tenant ID, and client secret).

  2. ABAP Code: The following code demonstrates how to upload a PDF file to OneDrive using the shared link and HDM.

    DATA: lv_url TYPE string,
      lv_token TYPE string,
      lv_file_path TYPE string,
      lv_file_name TYPE string,
      lv_shared_link TYPE string,
      lv_response TYPE string,
      lv_content TYPE xstring.

" Set your OneDrive shared link and file details
lv_shared_link = 'YOUR_ONEDRIVE_SHARED_LINK'.
lv_file_path = 'YOUR_LOCAL_FILE_PATH'.
lv_file_name = 'YOUR_FILE_NAME.pdf'.

" Get the access token (you need to implement the OAuth 2.0 flow to get the token)
lv_token = 'YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN'.

" Read the PDF file content
CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_upload
  EXPORTING
    filename = lv_file_path
    filetype = 'BIN'
  IMPORTING
    filelength = lv_file_length
  CHANGING
    data_tab = lv_content.

" Create the upload URL
lv_url = lv_shared_link && '/content'.

" Make the HTTP request to upload the file
DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_http_client=>create_by_url( lv_url ).
lo_http_client->request->set_method( 'PUT' ).
lo_http_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Authorization' value = |Bearer { lv_token }| ).
lo_http_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Content-Type' value = 'application/pdf' ).
lo_http_client->request->set_cdata( lv_content ).

" Send the request
lo_http_client->send( ).
lo_http_client->receive( ).

" Get the response
lv_response = lo_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).

" Check the response
IF lo_http_client->response->get_status( ) = '200'.
  WRITE: / 'File uploaded successfully'.
ELSE.
  WRITE: / 'File upload failed', lv_response.
ENDIF.

    Note:

    • Replace YOUR_ONEDRIVE_SHARED_LINK, YOUR_LOCAL_FILE_PATH, YOUR_FILE_NAME.pdf, and YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN with your actual values.
    • Implement the OAuth 2.0 flow to get the access token. You can use the cl_http_client class to make the necessary HTTP requests for the OAuth flow.

    This code provides a basic example of how to upload a PDF file to OneDrive using a shared link. You may need to adjust the code to fit your specific requirements and environment.

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Customizing of content form template in Output Management
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

To know more about this offering, please refer the blog:

Service with Advanced Execution

This blog showcases how the content form template can be customized to change the standard PDF generated by output control in service quotation.

Resource-Related Quotation

The resource-related quotation process begins when an execution order item is added in the service quotation. Once the execution order item is sent to planning by the service manager, it triggers the generation of a maintenance order. The maintenance order contains various operations and components based on planning, which are integral to fulfil the service requirements.

Once the planning is completed by maintenance planner, the planned data on the maintenance order is transferred to the service quotation via the DIP engine in the form of the resource-related cost line items. This are created as the subitems of main execution order item. These lines are distinguished from any other sub-items using the item category usage ‘RRCO’ (Resource-Related Cost).

To understand the process flow in Resource-Related Quotation, please refer the blog:

Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 3 – Planning and Cost Estimation in Service Quotation

Send to Customer

When the service quotation is sent to customer, the execution order item status is updated to ‘Released’. A standard PDF is generated by the output management which shows the details of the service quotation including items. This can be viewed in the Output Control assignment block. However, the resource-related cost items are not displayed in the standard PDF.

What is FDP?

FDP (Form Data Provider) is a gateway model that supplies the necessary data for generating output documents, such as invoices or purchase orders. It acts as the data source for a form template used in the output process, defining the structure and content of the document in the desired format.

What is output form?

Output form is a form which is used to display data in the PDF format to the customer where customer can see the items and subitems with respect to their pricing. 

Output form will read the data from FDP. 

Customizing Output Form 

The output PDF can be customized to enable display of resource-related cost line items as explained below.

Inside the form template, there is a provision to write scripts. The script must be modified to enable the resource-related cost line items in output form. 

The field SrvcQtnItmIsRsceReltdCostItm is already available in the node CRMS4S_SERVICEDOCUMENTITEMNODE for the OData FDP_CRMS4_SERVICE_QUOTATION.

This field value will be true if an item is resource-related cost item with item category usage as RRCO. This field will be consumed in the Script Section of the Adobe Print Form to suppress the RRCO line items in PDF output. The script will be edited using the Adobe Live Cycle Designer.

Step 1: Download the output form.

  • Go to t-code SFP (Form Builder).
  • Enter CRMS4_SERVICE_QTAN_SUMMARY in the form field.
  • Click on edit.
  • Click on Utilities -> Uploadingdownloading -> Downloading layout.
  • An XDP file will be downloaded.

Step 2: Modify the script in Adobe LiveCycle.

  • Open the XDP file in Adobe LiveCycle App.
  • In the Design view, the output format for the PDF can be seen.
  • In the rowTableSection -> rowTableItem, select the colItmCatUsg.
  • Open the Events with Scripts view in the Show option. The RRCO line items are suppressed in the output PDF by following logic. This code can be removed from the script to display the RRCO line items in output PDF.
  • Save the changes to the script.

Step 3: Upload the output form.

  • Go to t-code SFP (Form Builder).
  • Enter CRMS4_SERVICE_QTAN_SUMMARY in the form field.
  • Click on edit.
  • Click on Utilities -> Uploadingdownloading -> Uploading layout
  • Upload the modified XDP file.

After this step, when the service quotation is sent to the customer, the generated PDF will display resource-related cost items as well.

Summary

The standard PDF in the output control can be customized according to business requirement to enable or disable the display of resource-related cost items.

entry.title: Re: SAP Concur's Financial Integration Service explained.
entry.content:
Hello Tim,

Thank you for the detailed information on FIS.  We have a production issue with 2 cash advances from 2/12/2025.  The audit trail in Concur states that the posting was successful and shows an SAP document id.

FI Status changed from: PENDING to: SUCCESS, Message : Document Number: 1905599256 Posting Date: 2025-02-12

However, Doc 1905599256 is not a cash advance in SAP.  It is a completely unrelated vendor payment. And that document was created in SAP on 2/11, a day before.  There were NO cash advances created in SAP on 2/12.  Searches have been done on Doc id from JSON file and employee.

Furthermore, our feedback loops runs at 8:00 AM and 8:00 PM, the audit trail shows 02/12/2025 1:19 PM.  We do not show the feedback loop running at that time.

Are there any logs or audit trails we can review to track down how this could have happened?  This is a high priority for us and your expertise would be very much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: AI Core - Model Training and Inference for Multi outp...
entry.content:
Data Preparation

First, you need to prepare your data. Let's assume you have a dataset with both numerical and categorical features.

2. Preprocessing

You need to preprocess your data to convert categorical variables into numerical ones. This is done using encoders like OneHotEncoder for categorical features and StandardScaler for numerical features.

3. Model Training

Next, you create a pipeline that includes both the preprocessing steps and the model. This ensures that the same transformations are applied during both training and inference.

4. Model Inference

For inference, you need to preprocess the input data in the same way as during training, make predictions, and then convert the predictions back to their original categorical values.

entry.title: Deploy SAP Integration Suite - Edge Integration Cell - on Google Cloud Platform
entry.content:
What is Edge Integration Cell?

Edge Integration Cell (EIC), part of the SAP Integration Suite, brings a powerful hybrid integration runtime that bridges the gap between the cloud and your private landscape. It provides the flexibility to seamlessly manage APIs and run integration scenarios within a controlled environment, combining the best of both worlds.

With its hybrid deployment model, EIC makes it easy to design and monitor integration content in the cloud, while still allowing it to be securely deployed and run in a customer managed private environment.

 

Use cases and Advantages of EIC

  • Security or compliance use cases: Sensitive data stays safely within an enterprise's firewall, following strict rules to keep it within a private environment.
  • Migration path for SAP Process Integration customers: EIC helps customers design and monitor integration content in the cloud, while keeping deployment and execution fully within their private environments.

This blog offers a step-by-step guide to deploying Edge Integration Cell on GCP, with two setup options: a quick setup for trying out the simplest setup and a high-availability configuration for production. Whether you're looking for a quick test or a scalable, production-ready deployment, this guide makes it easy to configure SAP BTP and GCP resources. Once the setup is complete, the SAP Integration Suite can deploy integration flows securely to on-premise setup using EIC.

 

Architecture

Both the quick setup and the high availability (HA) setup architecture for deploying EIC on GCP utilize Google Cloud Kubernetes Engine (GKE). The quick setup uses the default Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) in the region, with all storage handled by GKE’s default storage classes and smaller compute and storage resources for a simple and efficient deployment.

In contrast, the HA architecture is spread across at least three availability zones for redundancy, with dynamic storage managed by the GKE cluster, along with additional services like Cloud SQL PostgreSQL for persistence and Memorystore Redis for caching.

On the SAP BTP side, the Edge Integration Cell must be activated within the SAP Integration Suite. Once the EIC is configured and successfully deployed on the GCP GKE cluster, integration flows can be designed and seamlessly deployed to the new EIC runtime, combining the powerful integration capabilities of SAP Integration Suite with the customer managed resources on GCP.

 

Edge Integration Cell setup overview on SAP BTP and GCP

Here is an outline of the steps involved in setting up and deploying Edge Integration Cell from SAP BTP environment to GCP.

Setup on GCP:

 

 Setup on SAP BTP:

You can find the detailed setup instructions in this GitHub repository.

 

Additional resources

Here are some additional resources

  • What is Edge Integration Cell | SAP Help
  • Deploy Edge Integration Cell on a custom server using SUSE Rancher
  • FAQ on Edge Integration Cell
  • SAP Integration Suite | SAP Help

 

Conclusion

I hope this blog has helped you set up Edge Integration Cell on GCP, making it easier to connect SAP on-premise solutions with the flexibility to design and monitor integrations in the cloud. By following these steps, you can build secure, high-performing business applications that blend innovation with control in a safe environment.

I want to express my gratitude to our SAP team members, Adarsh Hedge, Madan Pichamuthu, and Sivakumar N for their valuable contributions, guidance, and support. If you have any questions, feel free to contact paa@sap.com.

entry.title: Creating a Multi - input and Multi -output model
entry.content:
SAP provides a wonderful service over BTP DAR(Document Attribute Recommender ) which allows multi input and multi output model . However some Financial Companies handles extremely sensitive financial data, including personal information and transaction details.
They might be concerned about data privacy and security and are often subject to strict regulatory requirements regarding data handling and storage. 
In such a Scenario when they do not want to transfer any data over any other platform but make it in house using the following steps.

Let's understand each stage of the code to achieve this feature.

1. Data Preparation

First, you need to prepare your data. Let's assume you have a dataset with both numerical and categorical features.

2. Preprocessing

You need to preprocess your data to convert categorical variables into numerical ones. This is done using encoders like "OneHotEncoder" for categorical features and StandardScaler for numerical features.

Both OneHotEncoder and StandardScaler are available in Python through the scikit-learn library.

You can install scikit-learn using pip 

pip install scikit-learn

3. Model Training

Next, you create a pipeline that includes both the preprocessing steps and the model. This ensures that the same transformations are applied during both training and inference.

4. Model Inference

For inference, you need to preprocess the input data in the same way as during training, make predictions, and then convert the predictions back to their original categorical values.

So here  is the entire coding

import pandas as pd
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputClassifier
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier

# Example input data
X_train = pd.DataFrame({
    'Company_code': ['A', 'B', 'A'],
    'Vendor': ['X', 'Y', 'Z'],
    'Amount': [100, 200, 150],
    'InvoiceDocumenttype': ['Type1', 'Type2', 'Type1'],
    'Fiscalyear': [2021, 2022, 2021]
})

# Example output data
y_train = pd.DataFrame({
    'HKONT': ['Account1', 'Account2', 'Account1'],
    'KOSTL': ['Cost1', 'Cost2', 'Cost1'],
    'Profitcenters': ['Center1', 'Center2', 'Center1'],
    'Paymentterms': ['Term1', 'Term2', 'Term1'],
    'Partnerbanktype': ['Bank1', 'Bank2', 'Bank1'],
    'Taxcode': ['Tax1', 'Tax2', 'Tax1']
})

# Preprocessing for numerical and categorical data
preprocessor = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ('num', StandardScaler(), ['Amount', 'Fiscalyear']),
        ('cat', OneHotEncoder(), ['Company_code', 'Vendor', 'InvoiceDocumenttype'])
    ])

# Multi-output model
model = Pipeline(steps=[
    ('preprocessor', preprocessor),
    ('classifier', MultiOutputClassifier(DecisionTreeClassifier()))
])

# Fit the model
model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Example input for prediction
X_test = pd.DataFrame({
    'Company_code': ['A'],
    'Vendor': ['X'],
    'Amount': [120],
    'InvoiceDocumenttype': ['Type1'],
    'Fiscalyear': [2021]
})

# Predict
predictions = model.predict(X_test)

# Convert predictions to a readable format
fields = ['HKONT', 'KOSTL', 'Profitcenters', 'Paymentterms', 'Partnerbanktype', 'Taxcode']
prediction_dict = dict(zip(fields, predictions[0]))
print(prediction_dict)

entry.title: SAP Inside Track DFW 2025 - Dallas
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community from Dallas - FWT Texas.

We will be hosting the second SAP Inside Track event in DFW at the SAP Plano office location.
This event is a free event with a max capacity of 20 attendees.  

Register here

The main reason for organizing this event is to continue our community awareness within the DFW area, hopefully we can grow it into other cities and perhaps other states.

If you like to learn, network and if you work in S/4HANA, BTP, AI, Fiori, or any other SAP area, then this event is for you. We will also have food and drinks at the event as well. Don't miss the opportunity to participate.  See you there.

 

When:


Thursday, March 27th, 2025  3:00 - 6:00pm CST

 

Where:


SAP Office

7500 Windrose Ave. #250, Plano, TX 75024

Agenda

  1. Business Process Health – Ginger Lutrell
  2. SAP Learning Hub and SAP’s “Get and Stay Certified” Program – there are several recent announcements by SAP Learning designed to improved SAP developer certifications.  Riley Rainey will review the announcements and can answer questions about the new programs you might have.
  3. Experiments Leveraging Agent AI Capabilities inside SAP – recent developments in Agentic AI offer rich new capabilities for all developers.  Riley Rainey leads the Tutorial Subsystem team inside SAP. SAP Tutorial content is used by up to 20,000 developers a month via the SAP Tutorial Navigator. Riley will discuss fresh experiments that SAP is conducting to use agentic AI and LLMs for quality assurance in SAP’s tutorial subsystem.  This talk describes useful tips for using SAP AI Hub and open source resources such as Ollama, and AI agents like the “browser-use” project.
  4. Navigating a Complex Production Upgrade: A Story of Innovation and Adaptation by Surya Chavali
  1. Designing for AI: Moving Beyond Interfaces to Collaboration
    AI is changing the way people interact with technology, but great AI design isn't just about smarter interfaces—it’s about crafting meaningful collaboration between humans and AI. This talk explores key principles for designing AI-powered experiences that empower users, enhance trust, and create real value. (Virtual Presentation by Anshu Tank)

 

Sponsors:

If you or your company would like to help sponsor the event, please contact me directly on the SAP community. Thank you to the already sponsors for the event.

 

 

Thank you all and looking forward to seeing you at the event.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 Public cloud - Service - ATP check on service...
entry.content:
Hello,

ATP (Available to Promise) check functionality is currently not supported for service quotations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

The standard process proposed by SAP for handling service quotations in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is as follows:

  1. Create the service quotation manually without ATP check.
  2. If needed, check availability manually while creating the quotation as a workaround.
  3. Once the quotation is accepted, create a service order.
  4. Perform the ATP check in the resulting service order, where the functionality is fully supported.

Regarding future enhancements:

  • The specific roadmap for adding ATP functionality to service quotations is not planned.

For now, the recommended approach is to use the manual availability check as a workaround when creating service quotations, and then utilize the full ATP functionality once the quotation is converted to a service order.

 

Typically you do not want that Quotations already hard-book quantities in your MRP logic, because probability that a quotation gets an actual order is below 100% and you don’t take such commitments just because you are offering a service to a customer. 
 
If the quotation would already get confirmed ATP quantities, it would take away the stock for potential service orders that get in the system second later.
 
It follows the same principle as with regular Sales Quotations, also these business objects won’t reserve quantities unless they are taken over into an actual Sales Order.
 
Best regards,
Dequan Xu

entry.title: Overcoming SAP CPI Monitoring Limitations: Retrieving Attachments via OData API
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) provides robust monitoring capabilities through its Message Processing Logs (MPL), where users can track message processing and retrieve attachments. However, a common challenge arises when the SAP CPI monitoring UI limits attachment visibility to only the first 500 attachments, even when there are thousands.

This blog post provides a detailed guide on how to bypass this limitation and retrieve all attachments using the SAP CPI OData API.

Retrieving Attachments via SAP CPI OData API

          1.Identify Your CPI Tenant URL

For Neo Environment:

https://<tenant Id>-tmn.hci.<landscape>.hana.ondemand.com/

For Cloud Foundry Environment:

https://<tenant Id>.<system id, e.g. it-cpi001>.cfapps.<landscape>.hana.ondemand.com/

          2.Authenticate Your API Request

SAP CPI API requests require Basic Authentication or OAuth 2.0.

Using Basic Authentication, include your credentials as a base64-encoded username and password. Basic Authentication Headers:

Authorization: Basic <base64-encoded-username:password>

Accept: application/json

Or, if using Postman, you can put the username and password directly in the Authorization

If your tenant enforces OAuth 2.0, you first need to obtain an access token and use it:

Authorization: Bearer <your-access-token>

Accept: application/json

          3.Retrieve All Attachments

Use the following API request to fetch all attachments related to a specific message ID:

GET https://<your-cpi-tenant>/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs('<messageId>')/Attachments

The Message ID can be found in the SAP CPI Monitor section:

          4.View the Content of a Specific Attachment

Once you retrieve the list of attachments, each record will have an <id> field. You can use this id to download the attachment content by appending /$value to the request.

Example API Request to Download an Attachment

GET

https://<your-cpi-tenant>:<port(443)>/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs('<messageId>')/Attachments('<attachmentId>')/$value

Conclusion

While SAP CPI’s monitoring UI limits attachment visibility to the first 500 records, leveraging API capabilities allows you to retrieve and manage attachments beyond this restriction.

entry.title: Re: What is the future for Report and Statement Templates...
entry.content:
Hi, 
We have no plans to change from Jasper Report.

We do have functionality to test Statements from within the application that can be useful for Statement changes.

* Navigate to Statement Types and Select the desired Statement Type

*Select the Main Statement Template

* Select SHOW in the bottom right of the screen

* Select an existing Voucher # to test

* Click OK

A Sample Statement is created and is opened for you to review.

entry.title: How will GRC-Focused Consultancies adapt to the New SAP Partner Points System for Gold logo?
entry.content:
With the recent changes to the SAP Partner Points system, consultancies focused on Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) modules (e.g., AC, PC, RM, AM) are facing a significant challenge. The new framework no longer includes certifications related to GRC, and the points are now solely awarded for expertise in functional modules (BackOffice).

This leaves many GRC-specialized consultancies, like the one I work with, in a difficult position. It’s unclear how a consultancy focused exclusively on GRC can accumulate enough points to maintain its Gold Partner status under this new model. Does anyone have insights into how consultancies in this niche can navigate these changes? Are there plans from SAP to introduce GRC-related certifications or new ways for GRC-focused firms to earn the necessary points?

entry.title: Re: CR for VS dropped after 2027?
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

CR for VS follows the current version of Crystal Reports maintenance. If you follow that page you'll see the date gets extended every time a new release is available.

And as you know because I've posted it multiple times CR for VS only supports the current version, SP. Because SAP doesn't patch older SP's, full builds only, so if you find an issue it will get resolved in the next SP. As for the maintenance all of BI products are current version less 2 are supported.

Like all other software SAP releases patches to bring the product up to date with third party dependencies as soon as they can, some take longer than others due to implementation limitations.

Before I retired I was discussing the 32 bit version with R&D, due to ALL mother boards supporting 64 bit OS's there was no reason to install 32 bit versions of Microsoft Windows. Same as when 16 bit OS's back in the late 90's when 32 bit versions became available, yes I was supporting CR back then.... After CR 7 there was only a 32 bit version available.

So it's that time again to no longer maintain the 32 bit runtime, I believe SAP no longer even supports 32 bit versions of Windows.

As for your ERP programs, as noted in the other post, 32 bit will continue to run using the last SP that supports 32 bits but at some point you will need to move to 64 bit to keep up with supportable. I don't know how long VB 6 in compatible mode will continue to work, that's a Microsoft thing... You customers will need to upgrade.

And correct, as of December 2025 there will no longer be 32 bit runtime for distribution...

Don

entry.title: Re: What are the steps required after changing database h...
entry.content:
In a scenario where you want to change database hostname and services name, you can follow the steps below as a good practice:

First of, export the repository to .atl, as described in the KBA below:

  • 2768127 - Data Services repository migration steps - SAP Data Services

After that, follow the steps below:

  1. Open Data Services Server Manager
  2. Remove from Server Manager all the information related to the old database server
  3. After removing the old information and updating with the new hostname, create a new database and export the .atl repository

Hi,

Unfortunately, it is not possible to identify your specific issue without more detail.  It would be:

- The user getting the error does not have authoring permissions in Manager
- There is an issue with SSO and so the user getting the error is not identified as an Enable Now author
- SAP Enable Now Manager server settings may not be setup correctly
- The SAP Companion configuration is not complete or correct

Given that it could be a whole range of things, I would start with this post.  If after going through the post you do not have an answer, perhaps raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-CMP and get the support team to take a look.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Architecture overview of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)
entry.content:
SAP Business Data Cloud is a SaaS solution that combines SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud and their capabilities in one single solution. SAP Business Data Cloud is the path forward for SAP Business Warehouse customers. In this post we share a high level overview of the streamlined and robust architecture of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is built from interconnected components, each with specific technical roles:

Source Systems: Includes SAP (e.g., S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SuccessFactors) and non-SAP systems. Data is bundled into entities like tables/views (e.g., for working capital) and replicated to BDC.

Foundation Services: Replicates bundled data, harmonizes it with other sources, and transforms it into Data Products. Stored in a hyperscaler environment (SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake files), all integration and cleansing are SAP-managed. SAP will onboard LoBs progressively in SAP Business Data Cloud, starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

SAP Datasphere: Defines data models on top of Data Products. Supports prebuilt Insight Apps, custom models with ML/AI extensions, and manages roles and data access.

SAP Databricks: Extends analytics with AI/ML using an SAP-tailored Databricks component (called SAP Databricks). Data Products can be shared from BDC for processing and returned, or data can flow from Databricks to BDC, enhancing models with data science insights.

SAP BW/BW4HANA: Integrates legacy BW objects as Data Products. Supports SAP BW until 2030, with BW/4HANA recommended for modernization.

SAP Analytics Cloud: Delivers dashboards for Insight Apps, with visualization, planning, and AI-driven features (e.g., ad-hoc analysis, chat queries).

Insight Apps: Prebuilt SaaS apps for reporting and forecasting. Uses Analytics Cloud now, with SAP BTP-based apps planned.

BDC Cockpit: Manages the system—browse/install Insight Apps and Data Products, share data with Databricks for AI/ML.

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) delivers an AI-ready data solution by integrating SAP and non-SAP data into a unified semantic layer, powered by SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud. Paired with Databricks, it enables machine learning and automation via Delta Sharing, enhancing data usability. Hosted on SAP BTP, BDC connects processes, turns data into actionable insights, and supports scalable, AI-driven decision-making.

entry.title: Re: Different Papersize between CR Designer and RAS
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

Even though I'm retired I have no life so I still monitor the Community... lol

Have a look at this Blog I wrote about using custom paper sizes and the Zebra Printers:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/printing-crystal-reports-custom-paper-sizes/ba-p/13396188

I never noticed there was a difference in the User Defined size name between the Designer and SDK, but it's minor and I doubt it makes any difference.

In the blog above you'll note that when custom paper sizes are defined in Windows, Microsoft assigns a random ENUM to that new paper size, between R&D and myself I had them change the Paper size lookup order to look at the Name before the ENUM.

To explain the login look at a report that has not had the Page Setup used and then click on Print:

Notice there is no Printer name or paper size listed...

Once you select your custom paper size you will see both are updated:

NOTE: HS 0.93" is a paper size in the Brother Printer I have. I don't know if I can use define a custom paper size. Sorry I don't have the Zebra Printer installed on my home PC so I can specifically test it, but I'm sure if you define the paper name CR/.NET will use it.

So now that the Custom paper size is defined all you have to do is create that exact custom Paper Size name on every printer and CR will find it and use it. If CR can't find it it will look for the best fit, most of the time it works but not always... I don't recall if it look at every printer or maybe just the first few listed.

Sorry I don't have the Zebra printer connected to my home PC so I can't specifically test this but I do see what I saved in the RPT file I used for testing this:

So when using this the Dissociate doesn't really come into play.

If your users are not using Zebra printers CR should still look for the name, that's the key, always define your custom paper sizes using the exact same name, runtime should not have a problem.

As for the orientation, that's a known process, you'll see in my test app I always get and set the orientation, you need to do it also to set it, by default it always goes to portrait.... most times... depends on the printer actually...

FYI - SP 37 has rendering issues, I highly recommend upgrading to SP 38.

As for the RAWKIND, that's because you did not use the Page Setup, Windows defaults to 256, zero is not valid by the way. So by default Windows will assign a random ENUM above 127 I believe is the standard paper sizes  Microsoft defined.

So as I said above CR looks for the Paper Size name and then the ENUM, so on install let your users know then MUST define a specific Paper Size as defined in the RPT file. Once they do that everything will work as when created.

As for the only User Defined is likely due to you not specifically defining a custom paper size in Zebra, CR should fine their Defaults though, I don't know why it's not, other than you ignored the defaults and selected User Defined?

No other way to do this unless you want to do the paper size look up in code and set that size in your app. You'll have to figure out the logic to set the correct paper size and ENUM to use.

The Scientific printer driver, I don't recall the full name, it could be Seagull, anyways using that printer client it listed the actual ENUM assigned by Windows and you could change it using their interface.

To sum up,

  1. Define a specific Custom Paper size in the Printer
  2. Select and save the custom paper size in the In CR Designer Report. NOTE: Saving the report in .NET doesn't update this info, I don't think, possibly exporting to RPT format may save the updated info.
  3. Use that name on all installs

Have a great day Patrick

Don

And No I doubt anyone took over what I did, global support is now looking after paid support customers. I don't know if anyone is doing what I did....

entry.title: Re: SQL Anywhere Linux ODBC and TLS
entry.content:
I was finally able to get it to work from a Docker container. I did not have the correct cert for the db. Once I got the right cert I was able to connect and run a query. I am going to show my solution below in case someone else comes across the same issues. The Docker container needs to be cleaned up a bit. I am probably installing extra software I do not need.

Here is the ODBC.ini I ended up with: (The Encryption entry may be different depending on your cert and db start up params)

[ODBC Data Sources]
DataSourceName = SQL Anywhere 17

[DataSourceName]
Driver=/opt/sqlanywhere17/lib64/libdbodbc17.so
Description=Some Database
Host=23c8b.somedomain.com:2638
DatabaseName=databasename
ServerName=servername
UID=username
Integrated=NO
Encryption=TLS(trusted_certificates=*;company=23c8b.somedomain.com)
Password=password

Here is the odbcinst.ini file:

[SQL Anywhere 17]
Description=SQL Anywhere ODBC Driver
Driver=/opt/sqlanywhere17/lib64/libdbodbc17.so
Setup=/opt/sqlanywhere17/lib64/libdbodbc17.so

Here is the Dockerfile:

FROM ubuntu:22.04

# Prevent interactive prompts during package installation
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# First install unixODBC packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    unixodbc \
    unixodbc-dev \
    odbcinst \
    curl \
    gnupg2 \
    wget \
    ca-certificates \
    libncurses5 \
    build-essential \
    locales \
    inetutils-ping \
    openssl \
    tdsodbc \
    file \
    libaio1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set locale
RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
ENV LC_ALL en_US.UTF-8

# Create directories for SQL Anywhere
RUN mkdir -p /opt/sqlanywhere17/lib64
RUN mkdir -p /opt/setup_files

# This copies over the SQL Anywhere client install sqla17_client_linux_x86x64.tar.gz file. Download from SAP.
COPY ./sql_anywhere_client/ /opt/setup_files/

# Install SQL Anywhere client
RUN cd /opt/setup_files && \
    tar -xzf sqla17_client_linux_x86x64.tar.gz && \
    cd client17011 && \
    chmod +x setup && \
    ./setup -silent -nogui -I_accept_the_license_agreement

# Create wrapper script for odbcinst. This is a hack to get odbcinst to work with the system libraries.
RUN echo '#!/bin/bash\n\
# Temporarily set LD_LIBRARY_PATH to use system libraries for odbcinst\n\
OLD_LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"\n\
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"\n\
/usr/bin/odbcinst "$@"\n\
RESULT=$?\n\
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$OLD_LD_LIBRARY_PATH"\n\
exit $RESULT' > /usr/local/bin/odbcinst_wrapper && \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/odbcinst_wrapper

# Set SQL Anywhere environment variables
ENV SQLANY17=/opt/sqlanywhere17
ENV SQLANYSAMP17=/opt/sqlanywhere17/samples
ENV PATH=$SQLANY17/bin64:$SQLANY17/bin32:$PATH
ENV NODE_PATH=$SQLANY17/node:$NODE_PATH
ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$SQLANY17/lib64:$SQLANY17/lib32:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH
ENV ODBCINI=/etc/odbc.ini
ENV ODBCSYSINI=/etc

# Copy your ODBC data source configuration
COPY odbc.ini /etc/odbc.ini
COPY odbcinst.ini /etc/odbcinst.ini

# Register the driver with unixODBC using the wrapper script
RUN /usr/local/bin/odbcinst_wrapper -i -d -f /etc/odbcinst.ini

# Run ldconfig to update the shared library cache
RUN ldconfig

# Clean up setup files to reduce image size
RUN rm -rf /opt/setup_files
    
# Test connection with below
# ODBCTRACE=1 ODBCTRACEMODE=console isql -v DataSourceName username password
# Default command to start bash for debugging
CMD ["/bin/bash"]


There is a lot that can be cleaned up in the Dockerfile. Not sure if the wrapper script is even needed since the driver is hard coded into the odbc.ini file.

entry.title: Re: .NET Dynamic Report Assembly
entry.content:
Hi Nick,

Unfortunately there is no direct replacement between RDC and .NET SDK's

To get you started go her to get the latest SP 38 .NET Installer, note use the EXE to integrate into VS 2010->2022, you don't need to install the MSI packages the installer will do that for you. Redist packages are for distributing with your app:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

In that page are 2 samples I wrote:

how-to-parameters-in-crystal-reports-for-visual-studio-net

Printing Crystal Reports in .NET

SAP Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Installation Guide ( SP 21 and above)

What you need to use is RAS, it's an InProc Report Application Server. It has the ability to make changes to reports. FYI  - easiest way is to use a dummy report with everything in it and remove what you don't need.... of course there are caveats to the work flow using a default report...

You'll need to use .Clone() to copy the existing object, make the change and apply....

Yes unfortunately SAP disconnected a lot of the old Crystal Reports sites so a lot of the links no longer work.

entry.title: SAP Concur - Creating Invoice Purchase Requests via API
entry.content:
This is the second part of a series on utilizing SAP Concur Invoice APIs. Part one deals with vendor management and can be found here. This post will focus on creating purchase requests.

Prerequisites

You must be contracted for Invoice Processing, Purchase Requests for Invoice, and Client Web Services.

This post will not discuss authentication. Please see this post for an introduction to authentication and web service application building within Concur. Please use this link to download a Postman collection for the Invoice APIs. The complete documentation for the Purchase Request v4 API can be found on the SAP Concur Developer Center. Note that this collection assumes you’re using the environment from the authentication blog linked above. You will need an application with the following scopes:

  • purchaserequest.read
  • purchaserequest.write
  • INVPMT (needed to get the expense type codes sent in the purchase request payload)

Works with these SAP Concur solutions:

  • Invoice – Standard
  • Invoice – Professional

Background

Before we get into the nuts and bolts of the APIs, here’s a quick primer on the lifecycle of the requisition to payment process:

  1. An employee needs to buy a good or service. Before ordering / signing a contract, the employee must first seek approval to ensure the purchase is appropriate and is within budget. A purchase request is created, which contains a list of requested items, the quantity for each, and the unit price.
  2. The purchase request is submitted and will go through an approval workflow as designed by the customer. This could be a simple 1-up approval to the manager, or a more complex approval that goes to a cost approver depending on a cost center, project, or other cost object that was coded on the request.
  3. Once the purchase request is approved, a new purchase order is created based on the request details and is submitted to the vendor.
  4. The vendor receives the purchase order and ships goods / delivers services as needed.
  5. The vendor generates an invoice that’s sent to the customer. Note that you may also see invoices referred to as Payment Requests in some Concur documentation.
  6. The goods / services are received by the customer, and a goods receipt may be created indicating the quantity and date of receipt of the goods received.
  7. Accounts payable receives the invoice and matches it against the PO that was sent as well as the receipt—this is known as 3-way matching. Any discrepancies are noted and adjustments to the invoice and/or the PO  are made as needed.
  8. The invoice itself goes through an approval process. If the invoice matches the PO it can be configured to skip approval steps. If there were discrepancies between the invoice and the PO, then additional approvals can be required.
  9. Once the invoice is fully approved, it is released for financial posting and payment. Payment can be made directly from Concur via Invoice Pay or can be handled in an external system.
  10. Once payment has been confirmed, the payment status within Concur can be updated as appropriate.

SAP Concur Invoice has functionality to handle all these steps, but there are some scenarios where it may make sense to handle part of the process outside of Concur and use APIs to push the data to Concur instead.

Let’s say you already have an ordering system with a robust punch-out catalog, but maybe the approval functionality is limited and inconvenient for end users to use. In this case it makes sense to continue handling the order request creation process in your external system, but send the purchase request to Concur so you can take advantage of the flexible and easy-to-use approval process offered by Concur. In this scenario, step 1 from above will be completed by the purchase request API, and steps 2-10 will be handled through the normal Concur process.

Create a Purchase Request

Creating a purchase request is quite straightforward. The payload is separated into two parts: the header information that applies to the request as a whole, and one or more line items that details exactly what is being requested. Within the Postman collection, expand the Purchase Request folder and click on the Create Purchase Request row:

Line items do not all need to share the same vendor code or vendor address. If multiple vendors are used, note that a separate PO will be created for each distinct vendor code and vendor address combination.

The exact details that are required to be populated will vary based on your site’s configuration, but the payload in the sample Postman collection is typical of what’s needed. The complete schema details are here. Some of the values needed are also dependent on your configuration – here’s a list of those fields and where in the configuration you can find the configured values (you may need to work with one of your Concur admins and/or your Concur implementation team if you don’t have admin rights):

  • policyExternalId: Administration -> Invoice -> Policies – open the desired policy and scroll to the bottom:
  • shipToAddressCode and billToAddressCode: Administration -> Invoice -> Company Locations – use the value in the Address Code column:
  • vendorCode and vendorAddressCode: see my previous blog on vendor management.
  • expenseType: use the “Get Codes for Localized Strings” request in the Postman collection:
  • uomCode: Administration -> Invoice -> Units of Measure – Use the value in the Code column:

Once you have your payload built, you can send the POST request to create the purchase request. The response for a successfully created purchase request looks like this:

You can then make a GET request to the returned URI to see some basic details about the request:

 

Note that the approval status is “Pending Approval”. Purchase requests created via API are automatically submitted unless there’s a blocking exception or there’s no default approver assigned. In that case, the user will need to manually submit the request after clearing the exceptions or working with HR to have an approver assigned as needed.

At this stage, no further interaction with the request via API is possible. The Purchase Request endpoint does not support the PATCH / PUT / DELETE methods so you cannot make changes to a purchase request once it’s been created. When the purchase request workflow is completed, a new purchase order is created based on the details of the request and is transmitted to the appropriate vendor(s).

At this point, you can retrieve the IDs of the created purchase order(s) by making a GET request to the purchase request endpoint again:

You would then be able to use the PO numbers to call the purchase order endpoint in order to retrieve the PO details. I will cover those APIs in part three of this series, so stay tuned!

Share and Connect

What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.
Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.
Want to know more about SAP Concur? Please follow here.
Want to ask questions about SAP Concur and its offerings? Ask here.

Thank you!
